 {"id":"brenner_bot-1v26.4","title":"Implement Session Export/Import","description":"# Implement Session Export/Import

## Purpose
Allow users to export sessions for backup, sharing, or moving between devices.

## Export Formats

### JSON (Full Fidelity)
```typescript
interface SessionExport {
  format: 'brenner-session-v1';
  exportedAt: Date;
  session: Session;
  checksum: string;  // For integrity verification
}
```

### Markdown (Human Readable)
- Convert session to readable markdown
- Include hypothesis card, operator results, evidence
- Not re-importable (one-way)

## Implementation
```typescript
async function exportSession(
  session: Session,
  format: 'json' | 'markdown'
): Promise<Blob>;

async function importSession(
  file: File
): Promise<{ session: Session; warnings: string[] }>;
```

## Import Validation
- Check format version compatibility
- Verify checksum
- Validate all required fields
- Handle missing optional fields gracefully
- Return warnings for any issues (don't fail silently)

## UI Integration
- Export button in session header
- Import in session list
- Drag-and-drop import support

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/export.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] JSON export includes all data
- [ ] JSON import restores exactly
- [ ] Markdown export is readable
- [ ] Version mismatch handled gracefully

 {"id":"brenner_bot-1v26.5","title":"FEATURE: Personal Analytics Dashboard - Learning Patterns & Progress","description":"# Personal Analytics Dashboard - Learning Patterns & Progress

## The Gap This Fills
The system tracks behavior via GA4 for product analytics, but users have no visibility into their OWN learning patterns:
- How many hypotheses have I tested?
- What's my falsification rate?
- Which operators do I use most?
- Am I getting better at formulating testable hypotheses?

## Feature Design

### Personal Metrics
```typescript
interface PersonalAnalytics {
  userId: string;
  
  // Volume metrics
  sessionsCompleted: number;
  hypothesesTested: number;
  testsRecorded: number;
  
  // Quality metrics
  averageFalsifiabilityScore: number;
  averageSpecificityScore: number;
  hypothesesWithCompetitors: number;
  
  // Outcome metrics
  hypothesesFalsified: number;
  hypothesesRobust: number;
  hypothesesAbandoned: number;
  
  // Learning metrics
  objectionsAddressed: number;
  objectionsAccepted: number;
  revisionsAfterEvidence: number;
  
  // Process metrics
  operatorsUsedDistribution: Record<OperatorType, number>;
  averageSessionDuration: number;
  completionRate: number;
  
  // Trends
  trendsOver30Days: TrendData;
  trendsOver90Days: TrendData;
}
```

### Dashboard View
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  YOUR RESEARCH JOURNEY                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│  OVERVIEW                                                   │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│  Sessions: 23        Hypotheses: 31       Tests: 87        │
│  Robust: 8 (26%)     Falsified: 12 (39%)  In Progress: 11  │
│                                                             │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│  LEARNING PROGRESS                                          │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│                                                             │
│  Falsifiability Score Trend:                               │
│  [Chart showing improvement over time]                     │
│  "Your hypotheses are becoming more testable! ↑12%"        │
│                                                             │
│  Objection Handling:                                        │
│  Addressed: 45    Accepted: 12    Dismissed: 8             │
│  "You take objections seriously — good sign."              │
│                                                             │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│  OPERATOR USAGE                                             │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│  Level Split:      ████████████░░░ 78%                     │
│  Exclusion Test:   ███████████████ 100%                    │
│  Object Transpose: ████████░░░░░░░ 52%                     │
│  Scale Check:      ████░░░░░░░░░░░ 26%                     │
│                                                             │
│  "Consider using Scale Check more — effect sizes matter!"  │
│                                                             │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│  GRAVEYARD INSIGHTS                                         │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│  Most common death cause: Reverse Causation (42%)          │
│  "Many of your falsified hypotheses failed due to          │
│   reverse causation. Consider this earlier."               │
│                                                             │
│  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│  ACHIEVEMENTS                                               │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│  🪦 Hypothesis Hunter (5 falsified)                        │
│  🔬 Scientific Method (revised after evidence)             │
│  🎯 Precision Thinker (avg specificity > 80%)              │
│  🏆 Next: "Adversarial Mindset" (address 10 objections)    │
│                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Key Features

#### 1. Learning Curve Visualization
Show how the user's hypothesis quality improves:
- Falsifiability scores over time
- Specificity scores over time
- Time to formulate testable hypothesis

#### 2. Pattern Detection
Identify user-specific patterns:
- "You tend to skip Level Split — this may explain why 40% of failures are confound-related"
- "Your strongest hypotheses use all four operators"
- "You're faster at formulating hypotheses than average"

#### 3. Comparative Benchmarks
Optional: Compare to anonymous aggregate:
- "Your falsifiability score is in the top 20%"
- "Most users take 45 minutes for Full Analysis; you average 62"

#### 4. Personalized Recommendations
Based on patterns:
- "Try using Scale Check more often"
- "Your Object Transpose usage is low — reverse causation is your blind spot"
- "Consider the Adversarial Deep Dive template for your next important hypothesis"

### Privacy Considerations
- All analytics local by default
- Optional anonymized aggregate contribution
- Clear data export/deletion options
- No PII in stored analytics

## Why This is Critical
Without personal analytics:
- Users don't see their own growth
- Learning is invisible
- No feedback loop for improvement

With personal analytics:
- Progress is visible and motivating
- Patterns reveal blind spots
- Gamification drives engagement
- Meta-learning: learning about your learning

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dashboard shows key metrics
- [ ] Trends visible over time
- [ ] Pattern detection provides insights
- [ ] Achievements are tracked
- [ ] Privacy controls work

 {"id":"brenner_bot-1vu","title":"Web API: sanitize recipients list in session kickoff","description":"## Problem
`POST /api/sessions` validates that `recipients` is a non-empty array, but does not validate that each recipient is a non-empty string after trimming.

## Why this matters
First principles:
- Input validation should be precise at the boundary.
- Avoids sending malformed payloads to Agent Mail and producing confusing downstream errors.

## Root cause
`apps/web/src/app/api/sessions/route.ts` maps `recipients.map((r) => r.trim())` without filtering empties.

## Fix
- Normalize recipients: trim, drop empty entries, optionally de-dupe.
- Reject if the normalized list is empty.

## Acceptance
- `recipients: [" "]` returns 400 validation error.
- Valid recipients still send successfully.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-2em","title":"Build distillations comparison view","description":"# Task

Create the split-pane comparison view for viewing distillations side-by-side.

# Scope (Reduced)

This task focuses ONLY on the split-pane synchronized view. Other components are now separate tasks:
- Overview page: brenner_bot-0li
- Crosswalk table: brenner_bot-hog

# Split View Features

1. **2 or 3 pane layout** - User can choose how many models to compare
2. **Model selector** - Dropdown to choose which model in each pane
3. **Synchronized scrolling** - Scroll in one pane, others follow
4. **Section alignment** - Attempt to align similar sections

# Implementation

```tsx
<SplitComparison>
  <Pane model='opus' />
  <Pane model='gpt' />
  <Pane model='gemini' />
</SplitComparison>
```

# Sync Scroll Logic

- Use IntersectionObserver or scroll event
- Calculate relative scroll position
- Apply to other panes with same ratio
- Option to disable sync

# Responsive

- Desktop: 2-3 panes side by side
- Mobile: Tabbed view (switch between models)

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Split view renders 2-3 panes
- [ ] Model selection works
- [ ] Sync scroll functions correctly
- [ ] Responsive mobile fallback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:15:02.358741387-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:37:04.89367459-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T21:37:04.89367459-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented: Split-pane compare view with sync scroll, model selectors, mobile tabs at /distillations/compare","labels":["compare","distillations","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-2em","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0li","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:00.323253462-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-2em","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-igw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:47.723522564-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-2ho","title":"Bug: AgentMailClient should parse SSE (text/event-stream)","description":"Agent Mail contract allows SSE responses (Content-Type: text/event-stream).

Problem: AgentMailClient currently assumes JSON and JSON.parse()s the body, which fails on SSE streams.

Fix: In apps/web/src/lib/agentMail.ts, detect text/event-stream and parse SSE frames (data: lines). Return the last JSON-RPC payload result (or error) and keep behavior identical for application/json.

Add a unit test that stubs fetch to return an SSE payload with one or more JSON-RPC result events.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PurplePond","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:41:38.440876032-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T08:49:53.969462671-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T08:49:53.969462671-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented SSE parsing for AgentMailClient.call() + added unit tests","labels":["agent-mail","cli","reliability","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-2ho","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:41:38.466791215-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-7b6","title":"Add /corpus index page (doc listing + filters)","description":"# Task

Create `/corpus` index page that lists corpus documents with search + filtering **and** respects the content policy.

Key constraint:
- In **public mode**, only show allowlisted docs (see `brenner_bot-5so.7.6.3`).
- For restricted docs (e.g., full transcripts), either hide them or show a locked stub that requires lab-mode auth.

# Documents / categories

- Transcripts (restricted by default)
- Quote bank
- Metaprompts
- Distillations
- Model response batches

# Page layout

- Search bar at top
- Filter chips (All, Transcripts, Distillations, Metaprompts, Responses)
- Document cards in responsive grid

Document card fields:
- document title
- type badge
- brief description/excerpt
- word count / estimated read time
- access state (public vs locked)

# Implementation notes

- Prefer a curated doc registry (avoid arbitrary filesystem browsing).
- Ensure doc routing cannot be used to read arbitrary files outside the allowlist.

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Public mode lists only allowlisted docs (or locked stubs) and never serves restricted content.
- [ ] Search filters results.
- [ ] Type filters work.
- [ ] Links navigate to document viewer for allowed docs.
- [ ] Locked docs show a clear "how to unlock" UX.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-7lf","title":"Content: Define Brenner Operators glossary entries (14 terms)","description":"# Brenner Operators Glossary Entries

## Goal
Write glossary entries for all 14 Brenner operators. Each needs:
- term: Display name with symbol
- short: One-liner for tooltip
- long: Plain-English explanation
- analogy: "Think of it like..." for non-scientists
- why: Relevance to scientific discovery
- related: 2-4 related operator/concept keys

## Terms to Define

### 1. level-split (⊘)
Separate program from interpreter; message from machine.
Example: DNA (program) vs ribosome (interpreter); "chastity vs impotence" distinction.

### 2. recode (𝓛)
Change representation/coordinates to make problem tractable.
Example: 3D protein folding → 1D genetic sequence problem.

### 3. invariant-extract (≡)
Find what survives coarse operations; use physics to kill cartoons.
Example: What survives across species = fundamental; what varies = implementation.

### 4. exclusion-test (✂)
Design experiments that eliminate whole model families at once.
Example: "If overlapping code, adjacent amino acid pairs would be forbidden."

### 5. object-transpose (⟂)
Change organism/system until the decisive experiment becomes cheap.
Example: Switch from mammals to C. elegans for developmental biology.

### 6. amplify (↑)
Use selection, dominance, regime switches to get "across the room" differences.
Example: Choose systems where signal is 50-70% of total, not 0.1%.

### 7. cross-domain (⊕)
Import tools/encodings from other fields to break monopolies.
Example: Apply information theory to molecular biology.

### 8. paradox-hunt (◊)
Use contradictions as beacons; start where the model can't be true.
Example: "How can DNA be 1mm in a 1μm cell?" → folding mechanism.

### 9. exception-quarantine (ΔE)
Isolate anomalies explicitly without hiding or destroying coherent theory.
Example: Note exceptions in ledger; don't sweep under carpet.

### 10. dephase (∿)
Work out of phase with scientific fashion; stay in opening game.
Example: Avoid crowded fields; find problems others haven't noticed.

### 11. theory-kill (†)
Drop hypotheses aggressively when nature says no.
Example: "Don't fall in love with your theory; be willing to kill it."

### 12. materialize (⌂)
Compile stories into decision procedures: "What would I see if this were true?"
Example: Transform verbal theory into concrete experimental prediction.

### 13. diy (🔧)
Build what you need; don't wait for infrastructure.
Example: Brenner's crude apparatus that worked vs elegant apparatus never built.

### 14. scale-check (⊞)
Calculate; stay imprisoned in physics; filter impossible cartoons.
Example: "Can this actually work at the relevant scale and timescale?"

## Writing Guidelines
- Avoid jargon in definitions (recursive problem!)
- Use concrete examples from Brenner's work
- Make analogies accessible to educated non-scientists
- Reference transcript §n anchors where applicable

## Deliverable
Add entries to `apps/web/src/lib/jargon.ts` under "operators" category.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T00:58:12.236001616-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:18:26.996180699-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:18:26.996180699-05:00","close_reason":"Enhanced all 19 Brenner operators with transcript §n anchors and added 3 derived operators (Quickie, HAL, Potency-Check)","labels":["content","glossary"]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-7oto","title":"Task: Create Hypothesis CLI Commands","description":"# Task: Create Hypothesis CLI Commands

## Objective
Implement CLI commands for managing hypotheses: list, show, create, link, search.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
Add to `brenner.ts` CLI or create `apps/web/scripts/hypothesis-cli.ts`

### Commands

#### `brenner hypothesis list`
```bash
brenner hypothesis list [--state=<state>] [--session=<id>] [--format=table|json]

# Examples:
brenner hypothesis list
brenner hypothesis list --state=active
brenner hypothesis list --session=RS-20251230
```

Output:
```
ID                  | State  | Statement (truncated)
--------------------|--------|----------------------------------
H-RS20251230-001    | active | Cell fate is epigenetically...
H-RS20251230-002    | killed | Methylation is the only...
H-RS20251231-001    | active | Third alternative: chromatin...
```

#### `brenner hypothesis show <id>`
```bash
brenner hypothesis show H-RS20251230-001

# Output:
Hypothesis: H-RS20251230-001
Statement: Cell fate is epigenetically determined by methylation patterns
  established during early development.

State: active
Origin: proposed
Session: RS-20251230
Proposed by: BlueLake (codex-cli)
Created: 2025-12-30T10:00:00Z
Updated: 2025-12-30T10:00:00Z

Tags: epigenetics, cell-fate, methylation
Notes: Initial hypothesis from paradox discussion.

Linked Tests:
  - T-RS20251230-001: Lineage tracing with Cre-lox (designed)
  - T-RS20251230-002: Single-cell ATAC-seq (completed)

Parent: (none)
Children: H-RS20251231-001 (refinement)
```

#### `brenner hypothesis create`
```bash
brenner hypothesis create \
  --session=RS-20251230 \
  --statement="Cell fate is epigenetically determined" \
  --origin=proposed \
  [--parent=<id>] \
  [--proposed-by=<agent>] \
  [--tags=tag1,tag2]
```

#### `brenner hypothesis link <child-id> <parent-id>`
```bash
brenner hypothesis link H-RS20251231-001 H-RS20251230-001
# Links H-RS20251231-001 as a refinement of H-RS20251230-001
```

#### `brenner hypothesis search <query>`
```bash
brenner hypothesis search "epigenetic"

# Output:
Found 3 hypotheses matching "epigenetic":
  H-RS20251230-001: Cell fate is epigenetically determined... [active]
  H-RS20251231-001: Third alternative: chromatin accessibility... [active]
  H-RS20251230-002: Methylation is the only epigenetic... [killed]
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 commands implemented and documented
- [ ] `--help` works for each command
- [ ] JSON output format for scripting
- [ ] Error handling for invalid IDs, missing files
- [ ] Tab completion hints where possible

## Dependencies
- Task: Define Hypothesis Zod Schema
- Task: Implement Hypothesis Storage Layer

## Effort Estimate
~300 lines of code, ~3 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownSnow","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:43:21.002318303-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T03:03:29.033854803-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T03:03:29.033854803-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented hypothesis CLI commands: list, show, create, search, link, stats subcommands","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-7oto","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-lm3b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:00.550135621-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-7oto","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-4p5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:00.641604875-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-7oto","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8hv8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:00.672783022-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-7oto","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:40:43.149691558-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-80h","title":"Feature: Testing Infrastructure Setup","description":"Set up core testing infrastructure for the project.

## Scope
- Vitest for unit/integration testing (Bun-compatible)
- Playwright for E2E browser testing
- Test configuration files
- Test scripts in package.json
- Test utilities and helpers

## Philosophy (from user requirements)
- NO mocks or fakes - test real behavior
- Real implementations, real data fixtures
- Detailed logging for debugging test failures

- vitest.config.ts configured for Bun
- playwright.config.ts for E2E
- package.json test scripts
- Test helper utilities
- Logging infrastructure for test output","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:09:37.96653903-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:49:38.815114737-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T22:49:38.815114737-05:00","close_reason":"All child tasks completed: Vitest installed, Playwright configured, test utilities created","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-80h","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-o7a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:10:03.756411674-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
